body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Inner speech, mantra meditation, focused attention, semantic cognition, verbal repetition.

**Intensity of repetitive negative thinking in depression is associated with greater functional connectivity between semantic processing and emotion regulation areas.** — Psychol Med. PMID: 36043367. DOI: 10.1017/S0033291722002677. Repetitive negative thinking (RNT), acognitive process that encompasses past (rumination) and future (worry) directed thoughts focusing on negative experiences and the self, is atransdiagnostic construct that is especially relevant for major depressive disorder (MDD). Severe RNT often occurs in individuals with severe levels of MDD, which makes it challenging to disambiguate the neural circuitry underlying RNT from depression severity.
